compiled_state.title: Preplanning creates stories with empty descriptions — puts description in comments instead of creation command
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## What happened

When /dso:preplanning creates stories from an epic, the stories are created with empty descriptions. The actual description content (acceptance criteria, done definitions, etc.) is added as a comment after creation instead of being included in the ticket create command.

## Why this matters

1. ticket show output has an empty description field — the description is buried in comments
2. Jira sync may not pick up comments as description content
3. Sub-agents reading tickets see an empty description and have to parse comments for context

## Fix needed

1. /dso:preplanning should pass the description to `ticket create story "<title>" --description "<body>"` (or equivalent)
2. Verify that the ticket create CLI supports a --description flag — if not, add it
3. Verify that the ticket edit CLI supports updating description — if not, add it
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC). Preplanning skill creates stories without -d flag, then adds description as a comment.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
